[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] '''Ragab''' was a [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] who served as an [[Officer/Legends|officer]] of the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|navy]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Ragab served with the [[New Republic Second Fleet]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] and was the [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]] of the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Emancipator]]'' in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]].

==Biography==
[[File:Drayson-SWR.png|thumb|right|250px|Admiral Hiram Drayson, whom Ragab served under in 4 ABY]]
A [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], Ragab was an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|navy]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Ragab and two other Mon Calamari admirals—[[Kalback]] and [[Nammo]]—were responsible for the successful operations of the [[New Republic Second Fleet]], which was officially [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanded]] by Admiral [[Hiram Drayson/Legends|Hiram Drayson]]. In [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], Ragab was [[Battle of Paqualis|defeated]] by the [[Raptors]], elite [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] loyal to [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]], at<ref name="warfare" /> the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim's]]<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Paqualis system/Legends|Paqualis system]]. The defeat caused New Republic Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] to abandon an attempt to push [[Zsinj's Empire|Zsinj's forces]] further up the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|hyperlane]].<ref name="warfare" />

In that same [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]'', in which Ragab appears, to [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]].</ref> Ragab met Admiral Ackbar and [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] at a briefing prior to the [[First Battle of Borleias]]. During the battle, as much of the New Republic attack force began to retreat in the face of the defending [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Imperial Military/Legends|force]], the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] witnessed the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Emancipator]]'',<ref name="xwing" /> Ragab's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]],<ref name="CSWE 1">{{CSWECite|III|74|Ragab}}</ref> drifting toward [[Borleias/Legends|Borleias']] [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] north but also orienting itself as if it was going to jump into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Horn felt it indicated that Ragab was trying to decide if his [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|ship]] should retreat or stay at Borleas to fight.<ref name="xwing" />

==Personality and traits==
Ragab was a Mon Calamari with [[Silver/Legends|silver]] skin.<ref name="xwing" /> He was a capable admiral during his [[Time/Legends|time]] leading the Second Fleet.<ref name="warfare" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ragab first appeared in ''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]'', a [[1996]] novel written by [[Michael A. Stackpole]] as the first installment of the [[Star Wars: X-Wing (novel series)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] series of novels. Ragab is identified as an admiral in the novel<ref name="xwing" /> and in ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', a [[2012]] reference book by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]].<ref name="warfare" /> However, he is referred to as a [[Fleet Admiral/Legends|Fleet Admiral]] in Volume 3 of [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="CSWE 1" /> despite Volume 1 of the book simply referring to him as an admiral.<ref name="CSWE 2">{{CSWECite|I|231|Emancipator}}</ref> This article assumes the source identifying Ragab as a Fleet Admiral is incorrect.
